steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Recognize complementary angles
steps.content:
The problem states $R + S = 90^{\circ}$, so angles $R$ and $S$ are complementary. By the cofunction identity, when two angles sum to $90^{\circ}$:
$$\sin(R) = \cos(S)$$

steps.title: Apply the given sine value
steps.content:
We're given $\sin(R) = \frac{4\sqrt{2}}{9}$. Using the cofunction identity:
$$\cos(S) = \frac{4\sqrt{2}}{9}$$
That confirms A is correct.
